title: henckelia urticoides
intro: Henckelia urticoides, also known as Urtica henckelia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Himalayas and is often referred to as a "stinging nettle" due to its ability to cause skin irritation when touched. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and other conditions. In herbal preparations, Henckelia urticoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestion and relieve stress.
